title: Chapter 6 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Arrays, Hashes, and Symbols!

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of an Array in Ruby?
options: To store an organized, ordered list of data.
questions.explanation: Arrays are sequentially ordered lists of data.

questions.question: Which punctuation marks are used to define an Array?
questions.explanation: You use Square Brackets, e.g., `list = [1, 2, 3]`.

questions.question: In the array `colors = ["Red", "Blue", "Green"]`, what is the index of "Red"?
questions.explanation: Ruby (like most programming languages) is 0-indexed, meaning the very first item is at position 0.

questions.question: How do you grab the absolutely last element of an array without knowing how long the array is?
questions.explanation: A negative index counts backward from the end. `-1` is always the final item.

questions.question: Which array method adds a new piece of data to the very END of the list?
questions.explanation: The `.push` method attaches data to the back of the array.

questions.question: What is the shovel operator `<<` a shorthand for?
questions.explanation: `array << "dog"` acts identically to `array.push("dog")`.

questions.question: Which array method permanently removes and returns the VERY LAST item from the array?
questions.explanation: `.pop` "pops" the final item completely off of the list.

questions.question: What happens if you run `.each` on an Array?
options: It loops through every item in the list sequentially, passing that item into a block of code.
It counts how many items are in the array.
questions.explanation: The `.each` method iterates over arrays flawlessly.

questions.question: Arrays can hold different types of data at the same time (e.g., Strings, Integers, Booleans).
questions.explanation: Ruby arrays are incredibly flexible! `[1, "apple", true]` is completely valid.

questions.question: Why would you use a Hash instead of an Array?
options: Hashes hold numbers, arrays hold strings.
When your data needs specific identifiers/labels (Key-Value pairs) rather than just being an ordered list.
questions.explanation: Hashes are vital for structured data like `{:name => "John", :age => 22}`.

questions.question: Which punctuation marks are used to define a Hash?
questions.explanation: Curly braces denote a hash mapping.

questions.question: What is the 'hash rocket' operator used to connect a Key to its Value?
questions.explanation: The `=>` symbol is known in the Ruby community as the Hash Rocket.

questions.question: If you have `user = { "name" => "Alice" }`, how do you access her name?
questions.explanation: You pass the exact Key into square brackets to pull the attached Value back out.

questions.question: If the key `"score"` doesn't exist yet, what happens when you type `player["score"] = 100`?
options: It completely overrides the player hash.
Ruby instantly creates the new Key-Value pair inside the Hash.
questions.explanation: Assigning data to a key that doesn't exist will automatically add it to the Hash.

questions.question: What makes a Symbol (like `:name`) better than a String (like `"name"`) to use as a Hash Key?
options: Symbols are created in memory exactly once and reused, making them incredibly fast and memory-efficient.
Strings cannot be used as keys at all.
questions.explanation: Every time you type a String, Ruby recreates it in memory. A Symbol is created once and reused infinitely.

questions.question: How do you define a Symbol in Ruby?
options: Put an exclamation point at the end `!`
Put a colon at the front `:`
questions.explanation: A colon identifies a symbol, e.g., `:age`.

questions.question: Identify the 'Modern/Shorthand JSON-style' syntax for a Ruby Hash with symbols:
questions.explanation: The modern syntax drops the hash rocket entirely and places the colon at the end of the symbol name `name:`.

questions.question: When iterating over a Hash using `.each do`, your block must accept ____ parameters to catch the Key and Value.
questions.explanation: Because a Hash is composed of Key-Value pairs, you catch the data like `hash.each do |key, value|`.

questions.question: Which keyword lets you grab the keys out of a Hash in a loop?
questions.explanation: The first parameter grabs the Key, the second parameter grabs the Value.

questions.question: Is it possible to have an Array floating inside of a Hash?
options: No, arrays and hashes cannot mix.
Yes, a Value inside a Hash can be literally any data type, including a massive Array.
Only if the Array is empty.
Yes, but you can only put 3 items in the Array.
questions.explanation: Absolutely! It is extremely common to structure complex JSON data using Hashes holding Arrays holding Hashes.
